You know, when we talk about enthusiasm, I always say, you know, it's not that over the top crazy enthusiasm. It's bottled enthusiasm that sits just below the surface. And here's where it gets interesting because, yeah, you know, he's been through the straight line this guy. He's he said to me, I was using the straight line. Exactly before I knew I was using the straight line. I had lurked it through someone who'd lurked with someone else. We had the whole straight line thing, the old formula, right? And it was passed down from generation. And then when I actually found out that I saw that I bought your course, I would just took it to a whole new level, right? But I can't get they still have in trouble getting these guys to close. And so I wonder what it is. What is that one missing thing he can't essentially he's himself as a world class. We can't teach others to do the same. And I'll tell you exactly what that is great thought. And I think it will really help everybody understand how you get yourself to that next level. It has to do with certainty, the transference of certainty. And the fact that what I realized today is that I have a way of sounding absolutely certain about something, even when I'm using a tonality of sincerity or the reasonable man tonality. No, it's we talk about, you know, I say at sales, the highest level is the transference of emotion and the emotion transferring certainty, right? But here's the thing. There's a tonality for certainty, right? There's a tonality for certainty and there's a tonality for sincerity, utter sincerity and a reasonable man tone, right? Sound fair enough? But even when I use those tonalities while I'm in sales mode, there's still an underlying certainty that comes through that essentially sells the line that sells the tonality. And I use that word. That was like the way in actor says, you got to sell the scene the way a great actor that like a Leo de Caprio when he sells the scene, he has a way of his body language tonality and it's just his way is he sells the scene. He makes you certain that yes, you're certain that he really is sincere. So even when I'm using the tonality of sincerity, I need to make sure that you are absolutely certain I'm being sincere. There's an underlying theme of certainty that flows through the entire sale. So the point is that this high of level of using the straight line is when you have the ability to sound certain, even when you're sounding sincere, that your ascension is an underlying sincerity. Imagine this, you know, you have the three 10s, very simple, right? They have to trust and connect with you at a very high level, trust and connect with the company at a very high level and love the product. You have three 10s, right? And without those three 10s, those, you know, that absolute certainty to three 10s, it's virtually impossible to close, right? But what happens is so this also certainty about everything else along the way, in other words, the old ABC always be closing. Well, from the first words out of my mouth, I'm making them certain that I'm a person worth listening to. When I'm sounding sincere or using a tone of sympathy and empathy, I'm actually making them certain that I really am sincere, that I really am to have empathy. And I don't mean to manipulate them in the evil way because you really need to have, you need to feel this way. It really helps when you want to have empathy, you want to be sincere and you want to be reasonable. I'm not talking about manufacturing bullshit here. What I'm saying is, is making sure that as you're acting in a way that's congruent with how you feel that you're, there's an underlying thrust of certainty that permeates through the entire sale and never stops. That's what I do. So even when I sound like I'm so, it's an urge you or trust me or sincere, there's something that's an energy force. I never forget in my mind that I'm transferring certainty. I'm always being judged that I'm one stupid statement, one bad tonality, one wrong by language, away from breaking rapport and blowing all the certainty I've created, not just about the three tens, but all that intrasale certainty that allows me to move someone down the straight line from the open to the close. This is what I do when I work with someone, when I work with the sales force and I really dig into the tonalities and I make them go line by line, word by word. What I'm doing is, is I'm training them when I say, you're sounding good. When I say you sound good, it's not just applying the right tonalities at the right time. That's a big part of it. But there's an also, this is underlying baseline transference of certainty that starts from the moment you open up your mouth and it keeps going and going and going and never stops until you close the deal.
